title: Geldermalsen

infobox.value: (Dutch pronunciation: [[ˌɣɛldərˈmɑlsə(n)]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Dutch "Help:IPA/Dutch") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:Nl-Geldermalsen.ogg "File:Nl-Geldermalsen.ogg")) is a town and former [municipality](/wiki/Municipalities_of_the_Netherlands "Municipalities of the Netherlands") in the [province](/wiki/Provinces_of_the_Netherlands "Provinces of the Netherlands") of [Gelderland](/wiki/Gelderland "Gelderland") in the [Netherlands](/wiki/Netherlands "Netherlands").

["Postcodetool for 4191KH"](https://web.archive.org/web/20130921053543/http://www.ahn.nl/postcodetool). *Actueel Hoogtebestand Nederland* (in Dutch). Het Waterschapshuis. Archived from [the original](http://www.ahn.nl/postcodetool) on 21 September 2013. Retrieved 3 July 2014.

["Niet-westerse allochtonen per gemeente 2010-2040 - Nationale Atlas Volksgezondheid"](https://web.archive.org/web/20161002090500/http://www.zorgatlas.nl/beinvloedende-factoren/demografie/etniciteit/niet-westerse-allochtonen-per-gemeente-2010-2040). Zorgatlas.nl. Archived from [the original](http://www.zorgatlas.nl/beinvloedende-factoren/demografie/etniciteit/niet-westerse-allochtonen-per-gemeente-2010-2040/) on 2 October 2016. Retrieved 27 March 2014.

Sculpture 'Uil' in front of the Lingeborgh

**Geldermalsen** (Dutch pronunciation: [[ˌɣɛldərˈmɑlsə(n)]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Dutch "Help:IPA/Dutch") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:Nl-Geldermalsen.ogg "File:Nl-Geldermalsen.ogg")) is a town and former [municipality](/wiki/Municipalities_of_the_Netherlands "Municipalities of the Netherlands") in the [province](/wiki/Provinces_of_the_Netherlands "Provinces of the Netherlands") of [Gelderland](/wiki/Gelderland "Gelderland") in the [Netherlands](/wiki/Netherlands "Netherlands").

## Town of Geldermalsen

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Geldermalsen&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Town of Geldermalsen")]

The town centre of Geldermalsen contains a two-aisled [Gothic](/wiki/Gothic_architecture "Gothic architecture") church dating from the 15th century, with a [Romanesque](/wiki/Romanesque_architecture "Romanesque architecture") tower dating from the 13th century. The town contains two [windmills](/wiki/Windmill "Windmill"): De Watermolen, which was built in 1772, and De Bouwing, which was built in 1848. Located in the east side there is a graveyard.

Public high-school 'The Lingeborgh' is located in the west.

## Former municipality of Geldermalsen

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Geldermalsen&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Former municipality of Geldermalsen")]

The municipality of Geldermalsen was formed on 1 January 1978, when the former municipalities of [Beesd](/wiki/Beesd "Beesd"), [Buurmalsen](/wiki/Buurmalsen "Buurmalsen"), [Deil](/wiki/Deil "Deil") and Geldermalsen were combined. The municipality had an area of 101.73 km2 (39.28 sq mi) and was one of the largest municipalities in the [Betuwe](/wiki/Betuwe "Betuwe"). The municipality had a population of data missing in 2021. On 1 January 2019 it merged with [Neerijnen](/wiki/Neerijnen "Neerijnen") and [Lingewaal](/wiki/Lingewaal "Lingewaal") to form the new municipality [West Betuwe](/wiki/West_Betuwe "West Betuwe").

The river [Linge](/wiki/Linge "Linge") flows through the municipality and town of Geldermalsen.

## Demographics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Geldermalsen&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Demographics")]

Ethnic composition of Geldermalsen in 2010:

* [Dutch](/wiki/Dutch_people "Dutch people"): 91.7%
* [Black](/wiki/Black_people "Black people"): 0.9%
* [European](/wiki/White_people "White people"): 4.5%
* [Arabs](/wiki/Arab_people "Arab people"): 1.4%
* Other non-Western: 1.4%:[[3]](#cite_note-3)
